 172 | async def _async_scan(
 173 |     target: Path,
 174 |     only: str | None,
 175 |     skip: str | None,
 176 |     output: Path,
 177 |     verbose: bool = False,
 178 | ) -> None:
 179 |     """Run all configured linters and write results to a JSON file.
 180 | 
 181 |     Args:
 182 |         target: The file or directory to scan.
 183 |         only: Comma-separated list of linters to run, or None for all.
 184 |         skip: Comma-separated list of linters to skip, or None.
 185 |         output: Path where the JSON results will be saved.
 186 |         verbose: If True, prints a detailed report to the console.
 187 |     """
 188 |     console.print(f"[bold blue]Scanning {target}...[/bold blue]")
 189 | 
 190 |     config = Config.load()
 191 |     
 192 |     # Filtering logic: Args override config
 193 |     only_list = [name.strip().lower() for name in only.split(",")] if only else config.only_linters
 194 |     skip_list = [name.strip().lower() for name in skip.split(",")] if skip else config.skip_linters
 195 | 
 196 |     active_linters = list(LINTER_MAP.keys())
 197 |     if only_list:
 198 |         active_linters = [name for name in active_linters if name in only_list]
 199 |     if skip_list:
 200 |         active_linters = [name for name in active_linters if name not in skip_list]
 201 | 
 202 |     # Use cast to satisfy MyPy that we are not instantiating the abstract BaseLinter
 203 |     engine = Engine(
 204 |         linters=[cast(type[BaseLinter], LINTER_MAP[name])() for name in active_linters]
 205 |     )
 206 |     
 207 |     with Progress(
 208 |         SpinnerColumn(),
 209 |         TextColumn("[progress.description]{task.description}"),
 210 |         BarColumn(),
 211 |         TaskProgressColumn(),
 212 |         console=console,
 213 |     ) as progress:
 214 |         task_id = progress.add_task("[cyan]Running linters...", total=len(active_linters))
 215 |         def progress_cb():
 216 |             progress.update(task_id, advance=1)
 217 |         
 218 |         results = await engine.run_all(target, progress_callback=progress_cb)
 219 | 
 220 |     if not results:
 221 |         console.print("[bold green]No issues found! 🎉[/bold green]")
 222 |         return
 223 | 
 224 |     # Logical Sort: by file then line
 225 |     results.sort(key=lambda r: (str(r.file_path), r.line_start))
 226 | 
 227 |     # Per-linter summary
 228 |     from collections import Counter
 229 | 
 230 |     counts: Counter[str] = Counter(r.linter_name for r in results)
 231 |     
 232 |     table = Table(title="[bold red]Findings Summary[/bold red]")
 233 |     table.add_column("Linter", style="cyan", no_wrap=True)
 234 |     table.add_column("Issues Found", justify="right", style="magenta")
 235 |     
 236 |     for linter, count in sorted(counts.items()):
 237 |         table.add_row(linter, str(count))
 238 |         
 239 |     console.print(table)
 240 | 
 241 |     if verbose:
 242 |         for idx, result in enumerate(results):
 243 |             location = f"{result.file_path}:{result.line_start}"
 244 |             if result.col_start is not None:
 245 |                 location += f":{result.col_start}"
 246 |             console.print(
 247 |                 Panel(
 248 |                     f"[bold]{result.linter_name}[/bold] [{result.error_code}] "
 249 |                     f"[yellow]{location}[/yellow]\n\n"
 250 |                     f"{result.message}"
 251 |                     + (
 252 |                         f"\n\n[dim]{result.snippet_context}[/dim]"
 253 |                         if result.snippet_context
 254 |                         else ""
 255 |                     ),
 256 |                     title=f"Issue {idx + 1}/{len(results)}",
 257 |                     border_style="red",
 258 |                 )
 259 |             )
 260 | 
 261 |     # Serialize to JSON
 262 |     output.write_text(
 263 |         json.dumps([r.to_dict() for r in results], indent=2), encoding="utf-8"
 264 |     )
 265 |     console.print(f"\n[bold green]Results saved to {output}[/bold green]")
 266 |     console.print(
 267 |         "[dim]Run 'codereview fix' to get AI suggestions and apply patches.[/dim]"
 268 |     )

 295 | async def _async_fix(
 296 |     input_file: Path,
 297 |     target: Path | None = None,
 298 | ) -> None:
 299 |     """AI suggestion and interactive patch workflow.
 300 | 
 301 |     If the results file is missing and a target is provided, it triggers a scan.
 302 | 
 303 |     Args:
 304 |         input_file: Path to the JSON scan results.
 305 |         target: Optional file or directory to scan if input_file is missing.
 306 |     """
 307 |     if not input_file.exists():
 308 |         if target:
 309 |             console.print(
 310 |                 f"[yellow]{input_file} not found. Starting automatic scan of {target}...[/yellow]"
 311 |             )
 312 |             await _async_scan(target, only=None, skip=None, output=input_file)
 313 |         else:
 314 |             console.print(
 315 |                 f"[bold red]Error:[/bold red] {input_file} not found.\n"
 316 |                 "Please provide a target to scan: [bold]codereview fix <target>[/bold]"
 317 |             )
 318 |             return
 319 | 
 320 |     try:
 321 |         data = json.loads(input_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
 322 |     except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError) as exc:
 323 |         console.print(f"[bold red]Error reading {input_file}:[/bold red] {exc}")
 324 |         return
 325 | 
 326 |     results = [LinterResult.from_dict(item) for item in data]
 327 | 
 328 |     if not results:
 329 |         console.print("[bold green]No issues to fix.[/bold green]")
 330 |         return
 331 | 
 332 |     config = Config.load()
 333 |     console.print(
 334 |         f"[bold blue]Loaded {len(results)} issues from {input_file}[/bold blue]"
 335 |     )
 336 |     console.print(
 337 |         f"[dim]Using configured provider: {config.provider}:{config.model}[/dim]"
 338 |     )
 339 | 
 340 |     try:
 341 |         ai = create_ai_provider(config.provider, config.model, config.api_base)
 342 |     except ValueError as exc:
 343 |         console.print(f"[bold red]Error:[/bold red] {exc}")
 344 |         return
 345 | 
 346 |     # Logical Sort: by file then line
 347 |     results.sort(key=lambda r: (str(r.file_path), r.line_start))
 348 | 
 349 |     # Background AI Task Orchestration with Rate Limiting
 350 |     ai_semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(1)
 351 | 
 352 |     async def _wrapped_generate_fixes(res: LinterResult) -> list[AIFixProposal]:
 353 |         async with ai_semaphore:
 354 |             fixes = await ai.generate_fixes(res)
 355 |             await asyncio.sleep(0.5)
 356 |             return fixes
 357 | 
 358 |     # Launch all AI tasks immediately in the background
 359 |     ai_tasks = [
 360 |         asyncio.create_task(_wrapped_generate_fixes(result)) for result in results
 361 |     ]
 362 | 
 363 |     for idx, result in enumerate(results):
 364 |         console.print(
 365 |             Panel(
 366 |                 f"[bold]{result.linter_name}[/bold] error {result.error_code} at "
 367 |                 f"[yellow]{result.file_path}:{result.line_start}[/yellow]\n"
 368 |                 f"{result.message}",
 369 |                 title=f"Linter Error {idx + 1}/{len(results)}",
 370 |                 border_style="red",
 371 |             )
 372 |         )
 373 |         
 374 |         # Visual diff context
 375 |         if result.snippet_context:
 376 |             syntax = Syntax(result.snippet_context, "python", theme="monokai", line_numbers=False)
 377 |             console.print(Panel(syntax, title="Original Context", border_style="yellow"))
 378 | 
 379 |         with console.status("[dim]Asking AI for a solution...[/dim]", spinner="dots"):
 380 |             proposals = await ai_tasks[idx]
 381 | 
 382 |         if not proposals:
 383 |             console.print("[red]AI failed to generate solutions for this issue.[/red]")
 384 |             continue
 385 | 
 386 |         for i, prop in enumerate(proposals, start=1):
 387 |             syntax = Syntax(
 388 |                 prop.code_diff, "python", theme="monokai", line_numbers=False
 389 |             )
 390 |             console.print(
 391 |                 Panel(
 392 |                     syntax,
 393 |                     title=f"Option {i}: {prop.explanation}",
 394 |                     border_style="green",
 395 |                 )
 396 |             )
 397 | 
 398 |         # Non-blocking prompt so other AI tasks can continue in the background
 399 |         choice = await asyncio.to_thread(
 400 |             click.prompt,
 401 |             "Select an option to apply (1, 2, 3...) or 's' to skip",
 402 |             type=str,
 403 |             default="s",
 404 |         )
 405 |         choice = choice.lower()
 406 | 
 407 |         if choice == "s" or not choice.isdigit():
 408 |             console.print("[dim]Skipping...[/dim]\n")
 409 |             continue
 410 | 
 411 |         idx_choice = int(choice) - 1
 412 |         if 0 <= idx_choice < len(proposals):
 413 |             selected = proposals[idx_choice]
 414 |             applied = _apply_patch(
 415 |                 result.file_path,
 416 |                 result.line_start,
 417 |                 result.snippet_context,
 418 |                 selected.code_diff,
 419 |             )
 420 |             if applied:
 421 |                 console.print("[bold green]Patch applied successfully![/bold green]\n")
 422 |             else:
 423 |                 console.print(
 424 |                     "[bold yellow]Could not apply patch accurately. "
 425 |                     "Skipping...[/bold yellow]\n"
 426 |                 )
 427 |         else:
 428 |             console.print("[red]Invalid choice. Skipping...[/red]\n")
 429 |
